title: Public Records Request — Chicago Department of Finance Generative AI Records

communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

FOIA Officer
Chicago Department of Finance
City of Chicago
Chicago, IL

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Chicago Department of Finance's use of generative AI tools by department employees.

We are requesting the following records under 5 ILCS 140/3:

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by department employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing department employee use of generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to department employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The City of Chicago's Department of Technology and Innovation published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) that applies citywide, including to the Department of Finance. The roadmap acknowledges that many city employees have already begun using generative AI for work purposes and recommends integrating AI into the workplace. Finance department employees involved in revenue analysis, taxation, procurement, and budget planning may use AI chatbot tools for data analysis, report drafting, or correspondence.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Illinois's Freedom of Information Act explicitly enumerates "electronic data processing records, electronic communications, and recorded information of any kind" as public records. 5 ILCS 140/2(c). The Public Access Counselor within the AG's office has issued binding opinions confirming that electronic messages are public records. The City of Chicago's Department of Technology and Innovation published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) acknowledging that "many Chicago employees have already begun using Generative AI for personal and work purposes" and recommending that the city "provide access to preferred platforms and integrate AI into the workplace as quickly as possible." Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: Public Records Request — Chicago Department of Business Affairs and Consumer Protection Generative AI Records

communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Chicago Department of Business Affairs and Consumer Protection

Chicago, IL

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Illinois's Freedom of Information Act explicitly enumerates "electronic data processing records, electronic communications, and recorded information of any kind" as public records. 5 ILCS 140/2(c). The Public Access Counselor within the AG's office has issued binding opinions confirming that electronic messages are public records. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: Public Records Request — Chicago Department of Technology and Innovation Generative AI Records

communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

FOIA Officer
Chicago Department of Technology and Innovation
City of Chicago
Chicago, IL

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Chicago Department of Technology and Innovation's use of generative AI tools by department employees.

We are requesting the following records under 5 ILCS 140/3:

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by department employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing department employee use of generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to department employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Department of Technology and Innovation published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) recommending that the city issue employee AI policies, vet and provide access to AI platforms, and establish a center of excellence. The roadmap acknowledges that many city employees have already begun using generative AI for work purposes. CIO Nick Lucius was confirmed by City Council in May 2024, and Gene Leynes serves as the city's AI Lead and Enterprise Architect. DTI is the central IT department responsible for citywide technology strategy, data platforms, and AI governance.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Illinois's Freedom of Information Act explicitly enumerates "electronic data processing records, electronic communications, and recorded information of any kind" as public records. 5 ILCS 140/2(c). The Public Access Counselor within the AG's office has issued binding opinions confirming that electronic messages are public records. The City of Chicago's Department of Technology and Innovation published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) acknowledging that "many Chicago employees have already begun using Generative AI for personal and work purposes" and recommending that the city "provide access to preferred platforms and integrate AI into the workplace as quickly as possible." Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: Public Records Request — Chicago Assets, Information and Services Generative AI Records

communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

FOIA Officer
Chicago Assets, Information and Services
City of Chicago
Chicago, IL

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Chicago Assets, Information and Services's use of generative AI tools by department employees.

We are requesting the following records under 5 ILCS 140/3:

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by department employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing department employee use of generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to department employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The City of Chicago's Department of Technology and Innovation (DTI) published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) recommending that the city issue employee AI policies, vet platforms, establish a center of excellence, and integrate AI into the workplace. The roadmap acknowledges that many city employees have already begun using generative AI. CIO Nick Lucius was confirmed by City Council in May 2024 with a mandate to navigate emerging technologies including artificial intelligence (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/workforce/chicago-appoints-cio-to-newly-reformed-tech-department). Gene Leynes serves as the city's AI Lead and Enterprise Architect within DTI. Note: the Department of Assets, Information and Services previously housed the IT bureau; DTI was established as a standalone department in late 2023.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Illinois's Freedom of Information Act explicitly enumerates "electronic data processing records, electronic communications, and recorded information of any kind" as public records. 5 ILCS 140/2(c). The Public Access Counselor within the AG's office has issued binding opinions confirming that electronic messages are public records. The City of Chicago's Department of Technology and Innovation published a Roadmap for AI (see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/sites/chitech/home/roadmap-for-AI.html) acknowledging that "many Chicago employees have already begun using Generative AI for personal and work purposes" and recommending that the city "provide access to preferred platforms and integrate AI into the workplace as quickly as possible." Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.
